name: Chicken Congee
instructions:
1. Place the rice in a fine-mesh strainer and rinse under cold running water, swishing it around with your hand, until the water runs completely clear—this removes excess starch and prevents the congee from becoming too gummy. Drain well and set aside.

2. Turn on your multi-functional pressure cooker (such as Instant Pot) and select the Sauté function, adjusting to medium-high heat. Once the display reads "Hot," add the olive oil and swirl to coat the bottom. Pat the chicken breast dry with paper towels, then carefully place it in the hot oil. Brown the chicken thoroughly on all sides for about 8 minutes total, ensuring each side develops a golden-brown crust before flipping—this adds deep flavor to the congee. The chicken doesn't need to be cooked through, just browned.

3. Add a few tablespoons of the chicken broth to the Instant Pot and use a wooden spatula to scrape up all the flavorful browned bits (fond) stuck to the bottom—this prevents the burn warning and adds richness. Turn off the Sauté function by pressing Cancel.

4. Add the drained rice, freshly grated ginger, and the remaining chicken broth to the pot, stirring gently to distribute everything evenly.

5. Using a sharp knife, carefully cut the corn kernels from the cob in long downward strokes. Break the bare cob into 3 pieces—the cob will infuse the congee with sweet corn flavor. Add both the kernels and cob pieces to the pot.

6. Close the lid and turn the valve to the Sealing position. Select the Porridge function (or Manual/Pressure Cook on high for 30 minutes if your model doesn't have Porridge). The pot will take 10-15 minutes to come to pressure before the cooking time begins—you'll see the float valve pop up when pressurized.

7. When the cooking cycle completes, allow the pressure to release naturally for about 15 minutes—the display will count up from 0. After 15 minutes, carefully turn the valve to Venting to release any remaining pressure. Once the float valve drops, it's safe to open. Remove and discard the corn cob pieces.

8. Carefully remove the chicken breast from the pot using tongs—it will be very tender. Place it on a cutting board and use two forks to shred it into bite-sized pieces, pulling the forks in opposite directions. Return the shredded chicken to the congee and stir to incorporate. Taste and season with salt and freshly ground black pepper, adjusting to your preference. The congee should be creamy and porridge-like.

9. Ladle the hot congee into serving bowls. Top each bowl generously with thinly sliced spring onions for freshness, a sprinkle of toasted sesame seeds for nuttiness, and a drizzle of spicy chili crisp sauce for heat and crunch. Serve immediately while hot.
simpleInstructions:
1. Rinse rice until water runs clear, then drain.

2. Brown chicken on all sides in Instant Pot using Sauté mode.

3. Deglaze with broth, scraping up browned bits.

4. Add rice, ginger, remaining broth, corn kernels, and cob pieces.

5. Seal lid, cook on Porridge setting, then release pressure naturally.

6. Shred chicken, return to pot, and season with salt and pepper.

7. Serve hot, topped with spring onions, sesame seeds, and chili crisp.
